text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously risk-on. The real driver today is the ADP miss (98K vs expectations) - soft labor = less Fed urgency to stay tight, which historically gives tech and chips a modest tailwind. Nomura calling out chip-stock staying power from hyperscaler spending backs your core book directly.

Your holdings: All positions are HOLD/NEW with no scanner SELL flags - genuinely fine today. SOXL (+4.8%) is a 3x leveraged ETF that decays daily - on margin, a sharp reversal hits you twice as hard; stop at 200 is wide, watch it actively. SPAL is a brand-new, very volatile leveraged ETF - tiny, fast-moving, treat it like a live grenade; 1.91% gain is fine but size matters here. QQQI (+0.67%) is just doing its income job, no concern.

Watch: Friday's official June jobs report - if it's also soft, chips and growth names could catch a real bid. Watch NVDA price action around 198 as a real-time read on institutional chip appetite.

Bottom line: Your book is in good shape - protect leveraged positions with active stops and let the chip narrative work.
plays: SOXL: Stop at 200 is your line - on margin plus 3x leverage, a gap down through that level is a compounding disaster; consider tightening closer to 220-230 if you want more protection.
NVDA: Scanner flags a WATCH (7/12, grade C) entry 198.39-198.62, stop 197.35 - thin R at 1.4 and mixed timeframe bias means only worth a small probe if price holds that zone cleanly.

No new high-conviction buys today - ALAB (9/12, grade A) is the cleanest scanner setup but price is in daily premium territory, meaning you'd be chasing; wait for a pullback toward the 443-450 order block before considering entry.
